pre_hearing: Review all evidence exhibits and organize by topic
Prepare 3 copies of each exhibit (court, opposing, self)
Print and review hearing outline
Confirm hearing date/time with court clerk
File proof of service at least 7 days before hearing (2026-04-15)
Prepare binder with tabbed sections
Review applicable court rules and standards
Practice opening statement (2-3 minutes max)
day_of: Arrive at 990 Terrace Street, Muskegon, MI 49442 at least 30 minutes early
Check in with court clerk upon arrival
Dress professionally (suit/business attire)
Bring: photo ID, case file, exhibits, pen, notepad
Silence phone before entering courtroom
Stand when judge enters/exits; address as 'Your Honor'
Speak clearly and at moderate pace
during_hearing: State name and case number for the record
Present evidence in organized, logical sequence
Object promptly to inadmissible evidence (see Objection Cheatsheet)
Take notes on judge's comments and opposing arguments
Request clarification on any orders issued
post_hearing: Request copy of any written orders
Note deadline for any post-hearing submissions
Write summary of what happened while fresh
Identify any appeal issues to preserve

key_arguments.claim: 567-day denial of parenting time constitutes constructive restraint
key_arguments.elements: MCL 600.4301 habeas corpus; constructive restraint; parental liberty interest; void ex parte orders
key_arguments.supporting_evidence: Father denied ALL parenting time for 567+ consecutive days via ex parte orders lacking MCR 3.207(C) compliance

key_arguments.claim: Suspension of all parenting time without evidentiary hearing violates substantive due process
key_arguments.elements: Fundamental liberty interest; oldest recognized right; strict scrutiny for state interference; evidentiary hearing required
key_arguments.supporting_evidence: The Supreme Court in Troxel v Granville, 530 US 57 (2000) recognized that the liberty interest of parents in the care, custody, and control of their children is perhaps the oldest of the fundamental l...

FOC_caseworker.name: FOC Caseworker (Pamela Rusco)
direct_topics: Compliance with existing orders by both parties
cross_topics: How many home visits were conducted?
Did you interview the child privately?
What specific evidence of harm did you observe?
Were both parents given equal consideration?
Did you review all submitted evidence?

teacher.name: Child's Teacher / School Personnel
cross_topics: Have you observed the child with each parent?
Any concerns about either parent?
Is the child's performance consistent?

family_member.name: Family Member / Character Witness
direct_topics: Child's behavior during visits with plaintiff
Changes observed since custody issues began
cross_topics: Frequency of personal observations
Any bias or interest in the outcome?

objection_cheatsheet.when: Evidence has no tendency to make a fact of consequence more or less probable
objection_cheatsheet.response: Explain the logical connection to a material issue

objection_cheatsheet.when: Probative value substantially outweighed by prejudice, confusion, or waste of time
objection_cheatsheet.response: Argue probative value outweighs and offer limiting instruction

objection_cheatsheet.when: Witness testifying about matters they did not personally observe
objection_cheatsheet.response: Lay foundation showing witness's firsthand knowledge

objection_cheatsheet.when: Question suggests the answer on direct examination
objection_cheatsheet.response: Rephrase as open-ended question; permissible on cross

objection_cheatsheet.when: Out-of-court statement offered to prove truth of matter asserted
objection_cheatsheet.response: Identify applicable exception (803, 804) or non-hearsay purpose

objection_cheatsheet.when: Statement describing event made while perceiving it or immediately after
objection_cheatsheet.response: Foundation: show temporal connection to the event

objection_cheatsheet.when: Statement relating to startling event made under stress of excitement
objection_cheatsheet.response: Foundation: show the declarant was still under stress

objection_cheatsheet.when: Statement of declarant's then-existing state of mind, emotion, or physical condition
objection_cheatsheet.response: Show relevance of declarant's mental state

objection_cheatsheet.when: Statements made for medical treatment/diagnosis
objection_cheatsheet.response: Show statement was pertinent to diagnosis/treatment

objection_cheatsheet.when: Records kept in regular course of business
objection_cheatsheet.response: Foundation via custodian or qualified witness

objection_cheatsheet.when: Records of public offices setting forth official activities
objection_cheatsheet.response: Authenticate as public record

objection_cheatsheet.when: Proponent has not shown evidence is what it claims to be
objection_cheatsheet.response: Lay proper foundation (testimony, distinctive characteristics, chain of custody)

objection_cheatsheet.when: Copy offered instead of original document
objection_cheatsheet.response: Original unavailable; or duplicate admissible under MRE 1003

objection_cheatsheet.when: Evidence of person's character offered to prove action in conformity
objection_cheatsheet.response: Permissible exceptions: habit (406), motive, plan, identity (404(b))

objection_cheatsheet.when: Evidence of other crimes/wrongs to show character/action in conformity
objection_cheatsheet.response: Offered for permissible purpose: motive, opportunity, intent, plan, knowledge

objection_cheatsheet.when: Question calls for privileged communication (attorney-client, spousal, etc.)
objection_cheatsheet.response: Argue waiver, crime-fraud exception, or that privilege does not apply

objection_cheatsheet.when: Lay witness giving opinion not rationally based on perception
objection_cheatsheet.response: Foundation showing opinion based on firsthand knowledge and helpful to trier of fact

objection_cheatsheet.when: Witness not qualified as expert in relevant field
objection_cheatsheet.response: Demonstrate education, training, experience, and methodology (Daubert/Davis)

logistics.court: 14th Circuit Court, Family Division, Muskegon County

service_requirements.proof: File proof of service with court before hearing

objection_cheatsheet.when: Statement relating to startling event made under stress of excitement
objection_cheatsheet.response: Foundation: show the declarant was still under stress

objection_cheatsheet.when: Statement of declarant's then-existing state of mind, emotion, or physical condition
objection_cheatsheet.response: Show relevance of declarant's mental state

objection_cheatsheet.when: Statements made for medical treatment/diagnosis
objection_cheatsheet.response: Show statement was pertinent to diagnosis/treatment

objection_cheatsheet.when: Records kept in regular course of business
objection_cheatsheet.response: Foundation via custodian or qualified witness

objection_cheatsheet.when: Records of public offices setting forth official activities
objection_cheatsheet.response: Authenticate as public record

objection_cheatsheet.when: Proponent has not shown evidence is what it claims to be
objection_cheatsheet.response: Lay proper foundation (testimony, distinctive characteristics, chain of custody)

objection_cheatsheet.when: Copy offered instead of original document
objection_cheatsheet.response: Original unavailable; or duplicate admissible under MRE 1003

objection_cheatsheet.when: Evidence of person's character offered to prove action in conformity
objection_cheatsheet.response: Permissible exceptions: habit (406), motive, plan, identity (404(b))

objection_cheatsheet.when: Evidence of other crimes/wrongs to show character/action in conformity
objection_cheatsheet.response: Offered for permissible purpose: motive, opportunity, intent, plan, knowledge

objection_cheatsheet.when: Question calls for privileged communication (attorney-client, spousal, etc.)
objection_cheatsheet.response: Argue waiver, crime-fraud exception, or that privilege does not apply

objection_cheatsheet.when: Lay witness giving opinion not rationally based on perception
objection_cheatsheet.response: Foundation showing opinion based on firsthand knowledge and helpful to trier of fact

objection_cheatsheet.when: Witness not qualified as expert in relevant field
objection_cheatsheet.response: Demonstrate education, training, experience, and methodology (Daubert/Davis)

logistics.court: 14th Circuit Court, Family Division, Muskegon County

service_requirements.method: First-class mail or MiFILE electronic service
service_requirements.deadline: At least 7 days before hearing per MCR 2.107
service_requirements.proof: File proof of service with court before hearing

pre_hearing: Review all evidence exhibits and organize by topic
Prepare 3 copies of each exhibit (court, opposing, self)
Print and review hearing outline
Confirm hearing date/time with court clerk
File proof of service at least 7 days before hearing (2026-06-01)
Prepare binder with tabbed sections
Review applicable court rules and standards
Practice opening statement (2-3 minutes max)
day_of: Arrive at 990 Terrace Street, Muskegon, MI 49442 at least 30 minutes early
Check in with court clerk upon arrival
Dress professionally (suit/business attire)
Bring: photo ID, case file, exhibits, pen, notepad
Silence phone before entering courtroom
Stand when judge enters/exits; address as 'Your Honor'
Speak clearly and at moderate pace
during_hearing: State name and case number for the record
Present evidence in organized, logical sequence
Object promptly to inadmissible evidence (see Objection Cheatsheet)
Take notes on judge's comments and opposing arguments
Request clarification on any orders issued
post_hearing: Request copy of any written orders
Note deadline for any post-hearing submissions
Write summary of what happened while fresh
Identify any appeal issues to preserve

key_arguments.claim: 567-day denial of parenting time constitutes constructive restraint
key_arguments.elements: MCL 600.4301 habeas corpus; constructive restraint; parental liberty interest; void ex parte orders
key_arguments.supporting_evidence: Father denied ALL parenting time for 567+ consecutive days via ex parte orders lacking MCR 3.207(C) compliance

key_arguments.claim: Suspension of all parenting time without evidentiary hearing violates substantive due process
key_arguments.elements: Fundamental liberty interest; oldest recognized right; strict scrutiny for state interference; evidentiary hearing required
key_arguments.supporting_evidence: The Supreme Court in Troxel v Granville, 530 US 57 (2000) recognized that the liberty interest of parents in the care, custody, and control of their children is perhaps the oldest of the fundamental l...

best interests  of  the child  standard  under  MCL  722.23.

 changes  in circumstances,
 including
 parental  alienation,
 legal  manipulations,

anticipated_opposition.opposing_argument:
2.7 HARM/DAMAGES LEDGER (HDL)
Record-only harms (no speculation):
- parenting-time deprivation dates (if documented)
anticipated_opposition.rebuttal_theory: Record-only rebuttal candidate: demand pinpointed evidentiary basis; if conclusory label, flag missing findings/support and require on-record factual findings tied to admissible evidence.
anticipated_opposition.proof_needed: Transcript segment for basis; COE findings language; service/notice proof; contrary objective records (if admissible).

FOC_caseworker.name: FOC Caseworker (Pamela Rusco)
direct_topics: Compliance with existing orders by both parties
cross_topics: How many home visits were conducted?
Did you interview the child privately?
What specific evidence of harm did you observe?
Were both parents given equal consideration?
Did you review all submitted evidence?

teacher.name: Child's Teacher / School Personnel
cross_topics: Have you observed the child with each parent?
Any concerns about either parent?
Is the child's performance consistent?

family_member.name: Family Member / Character Witness
direct_topics: Child's behavior during visits with plaintiff
Changes observed since custody issues began
cross_topics: Frequency of personal observations
Any bias or interest in the outcome?

objection_cheatsheet.when: Evidence has no tendency to make a fact of consequence more or less probable
objection_cheatsheet.response: Explain the logical connection to a material issue

objection_cheatsheet.when: Probative value substantially outweighed by prejudice, confusion, or waste of time
objection_cheatsheet.response: Argue probative value outweighs and offer limiting instruction

objection_cheatsheet.when: Witness testifying about matters they did not personally observe
objection_cheatsheet.response: Lay foundation showing witness's firsthand knowledge

objection_cheatsheet.when: Question suggests the answer on direct examination
objection_cheatsheet.response: Rephrase as open-ended question; permissible on cross

objection_cheatsheet.when: Out-of-court statement offered to prove truth of matter asserted
objection_cheatsheet.response: Identify applicable exception (803, 804) or non-hearsay purpose

objection_cheatsheet.when: Statement describing event made while perceiving it or immediately after
objection_cheatsheet.response: Foundation: show temporal connection to the event

objection_cheatsheet.when: Statement relating to startling event made under stress of excitement
objection_cheatsheet.response: Foundation: show the declarant was still under stress

objection_cheatsheet.when: Statement of declarant's then-existing state of mind, emotion, or physical condition
objection_cheatsheet.response: Show relevance of declarant's mental state

objection_cheatsheet.when: Statements made for medical treatment/diagnosis
objection_cheatsheet.response: Show statement was pertinent to diagnosis/treatment

objection_cheatsheet.when: Records kept in regular course of business
objection_cheatsheet.response: Foundation via custodian or qualified witness

objection_cheatsheet.when: Records of public offices setting forth official activities
objection_cheatsheet.response: Authenticate as public record

objection_cheatsheet.when: Proponent has not shown evidence is what it claims to be
objection_cheatsheet.response: Lay proper foundation (testimony, distinctive characteristics, chain of custody)

objection_cheatsheet.when: Copy offered instead of original document
objection_cheatsheet.response: Original unavailable; or duplicate admissible under MRE 1003

objection_cheatsheet.when: Evidence of person's character offered to prove action in conformity
objection_cheatsheet.response: Permissible exceptions: habit (406), motive, plan, identity (404(b))

objection_cheatsheet.when: Evidence of other crimes/wrongs to show character/action in conformity
objection_cheatsheet.response: Offered for permissible purpose: motive, opportunity, intent, plan, knowledge

objection_cheatsheet.when: Question calls for privileged communication (attorney-client, spousal, etc.)
objection_cheatsheet.response: Argue waiver, crime-fraud exception, or that privilege does not apply

objection_cheatsheet.when: Lay witness giving opinion not rationally based on perception
objection_cheatsheet.response: Foundation showing opinion based on firsthand knowledge and helpful to trier of fact

objection_cheatsheet.when: Witness not qualified as expert in relevant field
objection_cheatsheet.response: Demonstrate education, training, experience, and methodology (Daubert/Davis)

logistics.court: 14th Circuit Court, Family Division, Muskegon County
